categoryDescription: Checks related to Gmail and email security configuration including authentication, routing, filtering, and data protection controls

checks.name: SPF Record Validation
checks.description: Sender Policy Framework (SPF) records must exist and be valid for all domains. SPF prevents email spoofing by specifying which mail servers are authorized to send email on behalf of a domain
checks.recommendedValue: Valid v=spf1 record published for all domains with -all or ~all qualifier
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Gmail > Authenticate email > Publish SPF record: v=spf1 include:_spf.google.com ~all for each domain

checks.name: DKIM Signing Enabled
checks.description: DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM) signing must be enabled and valid for all domains. DKIM provides cryptographic proof that email content has not been tampered with in transit
checks.recommendedValue: DKIM signing enabled with valid key published for all domains
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Gmail > Authenticate email > Generate DKIM key and publish DNS record for each domain

checks.name: DMARC Policy Audit
checks.description: Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting and Conformance (DMARC) policy must be set to reject or quarantine for all domains. A DMARC policy of none provides no protection against spoofing
checks.recommendedValue: DMARC policy set to reject or quarantine for all domains
checks.remediationSteps: Publish DMARC TXT record at _dmarc.<domain> with p=reject or p=quarantine. Start with p=none for monitoring, then escalate to quarantine and finally reject

checks.name: MTA-STS Policy
checks.description: Mail Transfer Agent Strict Transport Security (MTA-STS) prevents TLS downgrade attacks and man-in-the-middle interception of email in transit by requiring authenticated TLS connections
checks.recommendedValue: MTA-STS TXT record published and policy hosted at https://mta-sts.<domain>/.well-known/mta-sts.txt
checks.remediationSteps: Publish _mta-sts.<domain> TXT record with v=STSv1; id=<unique_id> and host MTA-STS policy file at https://mta-sts.<domain>/.well-known/mta-sts.txt

checks.name: TLS Enforcement
checks.description: Transport Layer Security (TLS) should be required for email transmission to prevent eavesdropping. Compliance TLS settings ensure encrypted connections with specified partner domains
checks.recommendedValue: TLS required for all outbound and inbound connections
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Gmail > Compliance > Secure transport (TLS) compliance > Add rule requiring TLS for all domains or specific partner domains

checks.name: Email Allowlist/Blocklist Review
checks.description: Email allowlists and blocklists should be reviewed for overly permissive entries. Allowlisted senders bypass spam filtering and can be exploited if misconfigured
checks.recommendedValue: Minimal allowlist entries with no wildcard domains; blocklist actively maintained
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Gmail > Spam, phishing and malware > Review Email allowlists and Blocked senders lists for overly broad entries

checks.name: Inbound Gateway Configuration
checks.description: Inbound email gateways should be properly configured to preserve sender authentication results. Misconfigured gateways can strip SPF/DKIM/DMARC headers or bypass security filtering
checks.recommendedValue: Inbound gateways configured with correct IP ranges and header preservation
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Gmail > Spam, phishing and malware > Inbound gateway > Verify gateway IPs and that authentication headers are preserved

checks.name: Email Routing Rules Audit
checks.description: Email routing rules should be reviewed for suspicious or unauthorized configurations. Malicious routing rules can redirect email to attacker-controlled destinations
checks.recommendedValue: All routing rules reviewed and documented with business justification
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Gmail > Routing > Review all routing rules, default routing, and recipient maps for unauthorized entries

checks.name: Auto-Forwarding Policy
checks.description: Automatic email forwarding to external addresses should be disabled to prevent data exfiltration. Attackers frequently set up forwarding rules after compromising an account
checks.recommendedValue: Auto-forwarding disabled for all organizational units
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Gmail > End User Access > Disable automatic forwarding for all OUs. Review existing forwarding rules via Gmail API

checks.name: Delegate Access Settings
checks.description: Mail delegation allows users to grant other users read and send access to their mailbox. Excessive delegation can lead to unauthorized access and impersonation
checks.recommendedValue: Mail delegation restricted and reviewed periodically; no unexpected delegates
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Gmail > End User Access > Review mail delegation settings. Check individual users for unauthorized delegates via Gmail API

checks.name: POP/IMAP Access Settings
checks.description: POP and IMAP access should be disabled unless specifically required. These legacy protocols bypass modern security controls and can be used for credential-based attacks
checks.recommendedValue: POP and IMAP disabled for all users
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Gmail > End User Access > Disable POP and IMAP access. Review individual user settings via Gmail API

checks.name: Spam and Phishing Filter Settings
checks.description: Enhanced spam and phishing filters should be enabled to provide maximum protection against social engineering attacks and malicious email campaigns
checks.recommendedValue: Enhanced spam filtering and aggressive phishing detection enabled
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Gmail > Spam, phishing and malware > Enable 'Be more aggressive when filtering spam' and all phishing protection options

checks.name: Enhanced Pre-Delivery Message Scanning
checks.description: Enhanced pre-delivery message scanning uses advanced heuristics and sandboxing to detect malware and threats before messages are delivered to user inboxes
checks.recommendedValue: Enhanced pre-delivery message scanning enabled
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Gmail > Spam, phishing and malware > Enable 'Enhanced pre-delivery message scanning' to identify suspicious content

checks.name: External Recipient Warning
checks.description: Users should be warned when sending email to recipients outside the organization to prevent accidental data disclosure and social engineering
checks.recommendedValue: External recipient warning enabled for all users
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Gmail > End User Access > Enable 'Warn users when they send messages outside the domain'

checks.name: Attachment Safety Settings
checks.description: All attachment safety protections should be enabled to detect and block malicious file attachments including encrypted archives, anomalous file types, and scripts
checks.recommendedValue: All attachment protection options enabled with quarantine action
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Gmail > Safety > Attachments > Enable all protections: encrypted attachments, scripts from untrusted senders, and anomalous attachment types

checks.name: Links and External Images Protection
checks.description: Link protection should be enabled to scan URLs for phishing and malware. External image proxying prevents tracking pixels and IP disclosure
checks.recommendedValue: URL scanning, click-time warnings, and external image proxying enabled
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Gmail > Safety > Links and external images > Enable 'Identify links behind shortened URLs', 'Scan linked images', and 'Show warning prompt for click on links to untrusted domains'

checks.name: Spoofing and Authentication Protection
checks.description: Spoofing and authentication protections guard against domain spoofing, employee name spoofing, and unauthenticated email from domains that appear similar to the organization
checks.recommendedValue: All spoofing and authentication protections enabled with quarantine action
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Gmail > Safety > Spoofing and authentication > Enable all protections: domain spoofing, employee name spoofing, inbound email spoofing, and unauthenticated email

checks.name: Compliance Rules Audit
checks.description: Content compliance rules should be reviewed to ensure sensitive data is appropriately handled. Rules can enforce encryption, quarantine, or rejection based on content patterns
checks.recommendedValue: Content compliance rules configured for sensitive data types with appropriate actions
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Gmail > Compliance > Content compliance > Review existing rules and create rules for sensitive content types (PII, financial data, health records)

checks.name: DLP Rules Configuration
checks.description: Data Loss Prevention (DLP) rules should be configured to detect and prevent sensitive data from leaving the organization via email. DLP provides automated content inspection and policy enforcement
checks.recommendedValue: DLP rules configured for key data types (credit cards, SSNs, health records) with block or warn action
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Security > Data protection > Manage rules > Create DLP rules for Gmail that detect sensitive content patterns and apply appropriate actions

checks.name: Gmail Confidential Mode
checks.description: Gmail confidential mode allows senders to set expiration dates and revoke access to messages. Review whether this feature is enabled or restricted per organizational policy
checks.recommendedValue: Gmail confidential mode enabled for users who handle sensitive data
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Gmail > End User Access > Review Gmail confidential mode settings and enable or restrict based on organizational requirements

checks.name: S/MIME Settings
checks.description: S/MIME provides end-to-end email encryption and digital signatures. If required by compliance, S/MIME certificates should be properly configured and managed
checks.recommendedValue: S/MIME enabled if required by compliance; certificates properly managed
checks.remediationSteps: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Gmail > End User Access > S/MIME > Enable hosted S/MIME if required and ensure certificates are uploaded and valid

checks.name: Mail Forwarding Rule Enumeration
checks.description: All user-level mail forwarding rules should be enumerated and reviewed. Attackers commonly set up forwarding rules to maintain persistent access to email after account compromise
checks.recommendedValue: No unauthorized forwarding rules; all forwarding rules documented and approved
checks.remediationSteps: Enumerate forwarding rules via Gmail API for all users. Remove unauthorized forwarding addresses. Disable auto-forwarding at the OU level to prevent future abuse
